vectorStoreId.description: Vector store the PDFs landed in.

documents.description: Per-file upload result, in input order.

isReady.description: True when every file has reached the `completed` state.

isFailed.description: True when at least one file is in the `failed` state.

fullName.description: Computed full name; null when both names are unset.

label.description: Display label fallback. Resolves to `fullName` when present, otherwise the email; safe to render directly.

imgUrl.description: Avatar URL. Defaults to a Gravatar identicon derived from the email when no custom image was uploaded.

assigneeId.description: Current assignee user id; null when unassigned.

anyOf.description: Slim user projection attached to records that include the user.

description.description: Free-text note attached to the status entry.

anyOf.description: Due date; null when no deadline is set.
Soft-delete timestamp; non-null for archived rows.

parentId.description: Parent activity id; null for outline-root activities. The same `parentId` axis chains outline nodes, their containers, and any subcontainers into one tree.

type.description: Activity type id. Schema-declared types (outline nodes and schema-defined containers) are schema-prefixed; `<SCHEMA_ID>/<TYPE>`, e.g. `COURSE_SCHEMA/MODULE`, `COURSE_SCHEMA/SECTION`. Content containers are not.

position.description: Position among siblings. Stored as `DOUBLE`; floats are allowed so callers can wedge a row between existing positions (e.g. 1.5 between rows at 1 and 2). The /reorder endpoint derives the stored value via `calculatePosition`.

data.description: Activity-level metadata bag (JSONB). Shape is owned by the schema's config for the activity's `type`. The display name for outline activities lives at `data.name` (typed as required in `@tailor-cms/interfaces/activity`).

refs.description: Cross-activity references (JSONB). Keyed by relationship type declared in the schema's `relationships` config for this activity type; values are arrays of `Relationship` pointers. Remapped on deep-clone via `mapClonedReferences`.

detached.description: True when an ancestor was deleted, leaving this row unreachable in the outline. The record itself stays intact.

isLinkedCopy.description: True when this activity is an active linked copy of another; receives automatic updates when the source changes. Editing `data` auto-unlinks via the model's `autoUnlinkOnEdit` afterUpdate hook.

sourceId.description: Source activity id this entry was copied from; preserved after unlink for provenance. Null when the source has been hard-deleted.

anyOf.description: Source `modifiedAt` at the moment of last sync.

isTrackedInWorkflow.description: True when the activity type appears in the schema's workflow configuration.

items.description: A workflow-status entry for an activity.

anyOf.description: Aggregated subtree last-modified timestamp.
Last publish timestamp; null when never published.
Soft-delete timestamp; non-null for archived rows.

location.description: External preview URL for this activity.

outlineActivityId.description: Closest outline-level ancestor of the linked copy; used by the FE to deep-link to the copy's location in the outline.

items.description: A entry-point linked copy of a source activity.

copies.description: Entry-point linked copies of this source activity.

repositoryId.description: Repository the session is scoped to.

mode.description: Autonomy level: `INSPECT` is read-only (the agent observes, plans, and asks); `EDIT` permits writes and deletions.

messageCount.description: Number of model turns in history.

transactionCount.description: Number of write operations logged across the session.

repositoryId.description: Repository the session is scoped to.

mode.description: Autonomy level: `INSPECT` is read-only (the agent observes, plans, and asks); `EDIT` permits writes and deletions.

repositoryId.description: Repository the session is scoped to.

mode.description: Autonomy level: `INSPECT` is read-only (the agent observes, plans, and asks); `EDIT` permits writes and deletions.

history.description: OpenAI Responses-API input array, accumulating across turns.

transactionLog.description: Write-operation audit log; entries are opaque transaction records.

kind.description: Discriminator from the canonical Entity vocabulary.

id.description: Target id. For embedded sub-elements (no row of their own), this is the PARENT element id; `embedUid` then selects the sub-piece.

embedUid.description: Sub-element selector when `id` points at the parent row.

name.description: Tool name as registered in the agent toolset.

input.description: Parsed JSON args the model produced.

name.description: Tool name as registered in the agent toolset.

input.description: Parsed JSON args, or - on `invalid_json` - the raw arguments string the model emitted.

result.description: Structured failure envelope returned by tools.

label.description: Picker label rendered to the user.

prompt.description: Prompt sent back to the agent when picked.

hint.description: Optional secondary hint under the label.

options.description: Answer options the user can click.

allowOther.description: Whether the picker shows a free-text "Other" row. true = open question with shortcuts; false = strict pick-one.

replyText.description: Assistant's reply text after the loop ended (the model's last text-only message). Empty string when the run produced no text.

items.description: Single tool-call audit row from a run turn.

toolCalls.description: Tool-call audit log for this turn.

turns.description: Number of (model call -> tool dispatch) iterations the loop ran.

truncated.description: True when the run was cut off (today only by maxTurns).

invalidates.description: Namespaced cache keys the client should refetch after this run (e.g. `activity:42`, `element:17`, `outline`, `assets`). Emitted by write-tools via their internal `_invalidates` field.

transactionLog.description: Cumulative write-operation log across the whole session.

pendingQuestion.description: Most recent `ask_user_question` call (if any); the dock renders it as a clickable picker above the input.

anyOf.description: Clickable picker the model can attach to a turn.

source.description: Search provider that returned the result (e.g. google, unsplash).

downloadUrl.description: Direct binary URL for downloadable content.

processingStatus.description: Current indexing state; null if never indexed.

vectorStoreFileId.description: OpenAI File API id once indexing completes; null until then.

storeId.description: OpenAI vector store id used for this repository.

assetIds.description: Subset of the requested ids that were eligible and queued for processing.

files.description: Map of fileKey -> stored attachment key (captions, etc.).

isCoreSource.description: Primary knowledge source for AI generation.

fileSize.description: Byte size of the stored file.

mimeType.description: MIME type returned by the upload.

description.description: Description collected from the target page.

files.description: Map of fileKey -> stored attachment key (captions, etc.).

isCoreSource.description: Primary knowledge source for AI generation.

url.description: Target URL of the link asset.

title.description: Title collected from the target page.

thumbnail.description: Preview image URL (may be empty string).

favicon.description: Favicon URL of the source domain.

altText.description: Alt text for image-style link previews.

uid.description: UID identifier; persists across clones / imports.

type.description: Asset kind (IMAGE / DOCUMENT / VIDEO / AUDIO / LINK / OTHER).

storageKey.description: Storage key; null for LINK assets.

publicUrl.description: Pre-signed access URL; only present when `signed=true`.

anyOf.description: Metadata for file-based asset types.
Metadata for LINK asset type.

meta.description: Type-specific meta for the asset; shape depends on `type`.

vectorStoreFileId.description: OpenAI id for the indexed asset; null when not indexed.

processingStatus.description: Vector-store indexing state; null when never indexed.

anyOf.description: Soft-delete timestamp; non-null for archived rows.

items.description: A repository asset, either an uploaded file (image, video, document, audio, ...) or an external link.
Page of rows.

total.description: Total rows matching the query (ignoring pagination).

url.description: Pre-signed download URL for the storage key.

AssetLegacyUploadResult.description: Resolved pointer to a stored file.

deletedIds.description: Subset of requested ids whose deletion succeeded.

url.description: Pre-signed download URL for the asset file.

uid.description: UID identifier; persists across clones / imports.

activityId.description: Parent activity id. Cleared to null when the parent element is removed, so the comment drops out of the activity-thread view but stays attached to its element for provenance.

contentElementId.description: Parent content-element id; null for activity-level discussion.

fullName.description: Computed full name; null when both names are unset.

label.description: Display label fallback. Resolves to `fullName` when present, otherwise the email; safe to render directly.

imgUrl.description: Avatar URL. Defaults to a Gravatar identicon derived from the email when no custom image was uploaded.

content.description: Comment body. The model's `len: [1, 2000]` validator caps the length. For soft-deleted comments the getter returns a "This comment has been deleted" placeholder; `deletedAt` is the authoritative deletion signal.

anyOf.description: Slim content-element projection attached to a comment.

contentElement.description: Eager-loaded element pointer; null when activity-level.

anyOf.description: Timestamp the thread was resolved at; null for open threads.
Last edit timestamp; null when never edited.
Soft-delete timestamp; non-null for archived rows.

type.description: Content-element type id, declared by an installed content-element extension package (e.g. `HTML`, `IMAGE`, `MULTIPLE_CHOICE`). Each package owns one type id; the elementRegistry dispatches lifecycle hooks and procedures by it. Capped at the model column length (`STRING` -> `VARCHAR(255)`).

position.description: Position among siblings. Stored as `DOUBLE`; the model column validator caps the range at 0..1_000_000, and floats are allowed so callers can wedge a row between existing positions. The /reorder endpoint derives the stored value via `calculatePosition`.

contentId.description: Origin id that survives clone/import so reuse and duplicates can be detected across repositories.

contentSignature.description: SHA-1 hash of `data`, recomputed on every save by the `processAssets` beforeSave hook. Used to detect duplicate content.

data.description: Content element type defined property bag (JSONB). Shape is owned by the content element package's `ElementData` interface for this `type`.

meta.description: Meta-input data bag (JSONB). Schema-configured fields collected via meta inputs (captions, labels, and similar element-level config).

refs.description: Cross-element references (JSONB). Keyed by the relationship types the schema declares for this element type via `elementMeta.relationships[]`; values are arrays of `Relationship` pointers. Remapped on deep-clone via `mapClonedReferences`.

detached.description: True when an ancestor activity was deleted, leaving this row unreachable in the outline. The record itself stays intact.

isLinkedCopy.description: True when this element is an active linked copy of another; receives automatic updates when the source changes. Editing `data` auto-unlinks via the model's `autoUnlinkOnEdit` afterUpdate hook.

sourceId.description: Source element id this entry was copied from; preserved after unlink for provenance. Null when the source has been hard-deleted.

anyOf.description: Source `updatedAt` at the moment of last sync.
Soft-delete timestamp; non-null for archived rows.

outlineActivityId.description: Closest outline-level ancestor of the source element; used by the FE to deep-link to the source's location in the outline.

outlineActivityName.description: Display name of the source outline activity.

outlineActivityId.description: Closest outline-level ancestor of the linked copy; used by the FE to deep-link to the copy's location in the outline.

outlineActivityName.description: Display name of the copy outline activity.

linkedAt.description: Timestamp the linked copy was created at.

items.description: An active linked copy of a source content element.

usages.description: Active linked copies of this source element across repositories.

key.description: Dimension key from the scoring rubric.

maxScore.description: Upper bound from the rubric definition.

rationale.description: Why this score, written to the author.

evidence.description: Short quotes or observations backing the score.

title.description: What the author did well, in one line.

detail.description: What to change and why it lifts the score.

targetElementId.description: Content element the suggestion concerns, when it targets one specific element; lets the editor scroll to it.

agentPrompt.description: Self-contained instruction the author can hand to the authoring agent to apply this suggestion.

rubricId.description: Scoring rubric that produced the review.

headline.description: One-sentence verdict naming the highest-impact improvement.

contentSignature.description: Content freshness key (activity `modifiedAt`) the review was computed against; compared against the live value to detect staleness.

computedAt.description: Unix ms when the review completed.

dimensions.description: Per-dimension scores keyed by dimension key.

computedAt.description: Unix ms when the snapshot was taken.

anyOf.description: A complete review of one activity against one rubric.

status.description: `empty` = never analyzed, `running` = analysis in flight, `ready` = result available, `failed` = last attempt errored (a previous result may still be present).

isStale.description: True when the content changed since the result was computed.

trend.description: Recent score snapshots, oldest first, current included.

state.description: Snapshot of the entity at the revision moment; concrete shape is determined by `entity`. Kept loose at the schema layer to avoid a hard dependency on the per-entity wire schemas.

user.description: Eager-loaded author of the change. Populated when the record is fetched with the User include (which is the default for list / get / time-travel actions).

updatedAt.description: Last update timestamp. Revisions are currently append-only, so this is typically == createdAt.

items.description: Append-only audit record capturing a single mutation to an Activity, ContentElement, or Repository.

activities.description: Activity revisions visible at the target moment.

repository.description: Repository context the AI generates against.

items.description: A repository activity (outline node, container, or flat collection item). The activity's `type` selects which schema-defined behaviour and `data` shape applies.

403.description: No access to the source repository.

sourceId.description: Source activity id (may belong to a different repository). The caller's read access is verified by `hasLinkSourceAccess` middleware before this fires.

parentId.description: Parent activity id; null for outline-root activities. The same `parentId` axis chains outline nodes, their containers, and any subcontainers into one tree.

position.description: Position among siblings. Stored as `DOUBLE`; floats are allowed so callers can wedge a row between existing positions (e.g. 1.5 between rows at 1 and 2). The /reorder endpoint derives the stored value via `calculatePosition`.

schema.description: Link payload; creates a linked-copy tree of the source under the target. Linked copies auto-sync from source until they are edited or explicitly unlinked.

get.summary: List activities in the repository
get.description: Returns the repository's activities in outline order.

schema.description: Restrict to activities with these ids.

Restrict to the activity with this id and all its descendants (walked recursively via `parent_id`). Used by the editor to pull a selected outline activity's container children (and any sub-containers) into the FE store without scanning the whole repository.
Include detached items (rows unreachable in the outline because an ancestor was deleted). Default: false.
Restrict to outline-level activities (those declared in the schema's outline structure). Adds the "published-but-not-yet-unpublished" carve-out so the FE can render pending-unpublish entries.
Set false to include soft-deleted rows.

post.description: Recalculates position from a sibling-index target.

data.description: A repository activity (outline node, container, or flat collection item). The activity's `type` selects which schema-defined behaviour and `data` shape applies.

403.description: Activity belongs to a different repository.

position.description: Position among siblings. Stored as `DOUBLE`; floats are allowed so callers can wedge a row between existing positions (e.g. 1.5 between rows at 1 and 2). The /reorder endpoint derives the stored value via `calculatePosition`.

post.summary: Deep-clone an activity into a target location
post.description: Copies the activity with descendants into the target.

items.description: A repository activity (outline node, container, or flat collection item). The activity's `type` selects which schema-defined behaviour and `data` shape applies.

403.description: No access to target repository.

repositoryId.description: Target repository id. The caller's write access is verified by `hasCloneTargetAccess` middleware before this fires.

parentId.description: Parent activity id; null for outline-root activities. The same `parentId` axis chains outline nodes, their containers, and any subcontainers into one tree.

position.description: Target position. Omit to keep the source activity's position.

schema.description: Deep-copies the activity (with descendants and content elements) into the target location.

patch.description: Removes soft-delete flag for activity and its descendants.

data.description: A repository activity (outline node, container, or flat collection item). The activity's `type` selects which schema-defined behaviour and `data` shape applies.

403.description: Activity belongs to a different repository.

post.description: Appends a workflow-status row; SSE and assignee email run as side effects.

data.description: A workflow-status entry for an activity.

403.description: Activity belongs to a different repository.

assigneeId.description: Assignee user id; null clears the current assignment.

description.description: Free-text note attached to the status entry.

dueDate.description: ISO-8601 due date; null clears the existing date.

anyOf.description: Due date; null when no deadline is set.

get.summary: Generate an external preview URL for an activity
get.description: Asks the configured consumer webhook for a preview URL.

502.description: Preview webhook returned no usable URL.

data.description: A repository activity (outline node, container, or flat collection item). The activity's `type` selects which schema-defined behaviour and `data` shape applies.

data.description: A repository activity (outline node, container, or flat collection item). The activity's `type` selects which schema-defined behaviour and `data` shape applies.

get.summary: List agent sessions

200.description: Sessions for the (user, repository) pair, each with derived counts.

items.description: Summary + derived counts (turns, transactions); returned by list.

data.description: Lightweight agent-session row (no history / transaction log).

mode.description: Autonomy level for the session; defaults to `EDIT`.

schema.description: Optional mode selection for the new session.

get.summary: Get an agent session

200.description: Full session payload (summary + history + transaction log).

data.description: Full agent session payload (summary + history + transaction log).

403.description: Session belongs to a different user.

404.description: Session not found in this repository.

403.description: Session belongs to a different user.

404.description: Session not found in this repository.

post.description: Resumes or creates a session and dispatches one model + tool round-trip; returns the reply, tool log, and Frontend invalidation hints.

200.description: Reply text, tool-call audit log, frontend invalidation hints, and an optional clickable picker the model attached to the turn.

data.description: Result of a single agent turn.

sessionId.description: Existing session to append a turn to. Omit to start a fresh session (the response carries the new `sessionId`).

mode.description: Per-turn mode override; lets the dock honour the current picker selection on resumed conversations.

focus.description: Editor focus context. The agent uses this to resolve pronouns without extra reads.

reasoningEffort.description: Reasoning effort. Honoured only when the configured model accepts `reasoning.effort` (gpt-5 / o-series).

post.summary: Search the web for repository relevant resources
post.description: Routes the query across Serper endpoints (web / image / news / video / scholar) and Unsplash based on the contentFilter; falls back to OpenAI web_search when Serper is unavailable or quota exceeded.

200.description: Deduped & trimmed search results from the providers.

items.description: A single search hit returned by the discovery service.

503.description: Discovery sub-service is disabled by config.

contentFilter.description: Restrict results to a content category; one of ALL, ARTICLE, PDF, IMAGE, VIDEO, RESEARCH, OTHER.

count.description: Maximum number of results to return (1..100).

schema.description: Search payload for cross-provider asset discovery.

get.summary: List indexing status for all repository assets
get.description: Returns indexing status rows for every asset that has been (or is being) indexed.

200.description: Status rows for every previously indexed asset.

items.description: One asset's vector-store indexing state.

post.description: Queues eligible assets (not yet indexed, failed, or stuck) and returns the store id plus the ids that were actually queued. Indexing runs in the background.

data.description: Acknowledgement of an indexing job, returned synchronously.

404.description: No eligible assets matched the selector.

assetIds.description: Asset ids to enqueue for vector-store indexing (1..100).

schema.description: Selector for the assets to index.

get.summary: Get indexing status for one asset
get.description: Returns the status row for a single asset; empty when the asset has never been indexed.

200.description: Asset status row, or empty when never indexed.

items.description: One asset's vector-store indexing state.

404.description: Asset not found in this repository.

delete.description: Removes the asset's file from the OpenAI vector store and clears `processingStatus` / `vectorStoreFileId` locally.

data.description: A repository asset, either an uploaded file (image, video, document, audio, ...) or an external link.

404.description: Asset not found in this repository.

get.summary: List assets or resolve a storage key
get.description: Returns a paginated list of repository assets in the default mode, or a `{ url }` signed-URL response when `?key=<storageKey>` is set.

200.description: Paginated list of assets, OR a key-resolve URL when `?key=` is set.

data.description: Paginated asset list (default mode).

anyOf.description: Storage-key resolve response (when `?key=` is set).

post.description: Multipart upload. Use the `files[]` field for asset-library uploads (returns the standard data envelope) or the legacy `file` field for content-element single-file uploads (returns the storage-key shape).

200.description: Library data envelope OR legacy storage-key shape.

items.description: A repository asset, either an uploaded file (image, video, document, audio, ...) or an external link.

anyOf.description: Library upload response shape, with an array of created Assets.
Legacy single-file upload response shape.

schema.description: Library upload response OR legacy single-file upload response.

400.description: No files provided in the multipart body.

post.summary: Create an asset from a URL
post.description: Collects OG metadata; for IMAGE / PDF content types also downloads and stores the binary. SSRF-guarded at both validation and execution.

data.description: A repository asset, either an uploaded file (image, video, document, audio, ...) or an external link.

contentType.description: Discovery content type (one of ARTICLE, PDF, IMAGE, VIDEO, RESEARCH, OTHER); drives whether the import downloads the file or stores as a link.

downloadUrl.description: Explicit binary URL when different from the page URL (e.g. CDN URL).

meta.description: Optional metadata; merged with Open Graph metadata.

schema.description: Create an asset from a URL.

post.description: Soft-deletes each asset by id. Failures are logged and the request never partials out — the response contains the ids that did delete.

200.description: Subset of requested ids whose deletion succeeded.

data.description: Successful deletions from a bulk-remove call.

assetIds.description: Numeric ids of the assets to soft-delete (at least one).

get.summary: Get a signed download URL for an asset
get.description: Only applies to assets with a stored file (not LINK).

200.description: Signed URL for the asset file.

data.description: Signed url for an asset download request.

400.description: Asset has no downloadable file (LINK type).

404.description: Asset not found in this repository.

post.summary: Attach a supplementary file to an asset
post.description: Stores the multipart `file` under `meta.files[fileKey]`. Replaces any previously attached file at the same slot; the old file is left in storage since published content may still reference it.

200.description: Asset row with the updated `meta.files` map.

data.description: A repository asset, either an uploaded file (image, video, document, audio, ...) or an external link.

400.description: No file provided in the multipart body.

404.description: Asset not found in this repository.

fileKey.description: Key (property) name inside `meta.files` (e.g. `captions`). Replaces any prior file stored under this key.

schema.description: Multipart payload for the attach-file endpoint.

patch.description: Patches editable meta fields. Unknown keys (including `files`) are silently dropped.

data.description: A repository asset, either an uploaded file (image, video, document, audio, ...) or an external link.

404.description: Asset not found in this repository.

isCoreSource.description: Marks this asset as a primary knowledge source for AI generation.

schema.description: Patch payload for an asset update operation.

delete.description: Soft-deletes the asset row and removes it from the vector store (if applicable). The underlying storage file is kept so content references stay valid.

data.description: A repository asset, either an uploaded file (image, video, document, audio, ...) or an external link.

404.description: Asset not found in this repository.

post.summary: Toggle the resolved state of a comment or element thread
post.description: Toggle the resolved state for a single comment or entire thread.

contentElementId.description: Bulk selector; every comment attached to the given element is toggled together.

resolvedAt.description: Current resolution state echoed back to flip: truthy -> unresolve (set resolvedAt to null), absent / null -> resolve (stamp resolvedAt = now).

schema.description: Toggle the resolved state of a single comment (by `id`) or every comment attached to a content element (by `contentElementId`). Exactly one selector is required.

get.summary: List comments in the repository
get.description: Returns the repository's comments in newest-first order.

schema.description: Restrict to comments on a single activity (the activity-thread view).
Restrict to comments on a single content element (the inline thread). May be combined with `activityId`; the client typically sends one or the other.

Set false to include soft-deleted comments (default: true). The list route's default already opts into `paranoid: false` so deleted comments stay visible in the thread (their body renders as a placeholder via the model getter); this flag is exposed for completeness.

200.description: List of comments matching the filter.

items.description: A repository comment. Either an activity-level discussion or an inline thread attached to a specific content element.

post.description: Creates a comment under the scoped repository.

data.description: A repository comment. Either an activity-level discussion or an inline thread attached to a specific content element.

uid.description: UID identifier; persists across clones / imports.

activityId.description: Activity this comment belongs to; required for element-scoped comments so the activity-thread view sees them (outline activity id).

content.description: Comment body. The model's `len: [1, 2000]` validator caps the length. For soft-deleted comments the getter returns a "This comment has been deleted" placeholder; `deletedAt` is the authoritative deletion signal.

patch.description: Replaces the body of an existing comment.

data.description: A repository comment. Either an activity-level discussion or an inline thread attached to a specific content element.

403.description: Caller is not the comment author.

content.description: Comment body. The model's `len: [1, 2000]` validator caps the length. For soft-deleted comments the getter returns a "This comment has been deleted" placeholder; `deletedAt` is the authoritative deletion signal.

delete.description: Soft-deletes the comment; the row stays for provenance.

post.description: Creates a linked copy of the source element.

200.description: Linked copy created in the target repository.

403.description: No access to the source repository.

sourceId.description: Source element id (may belong to a different repository). The caller's read access is verified by `hasLinkSourceAccess` middleware before this fires.

position.description: Position among siblings. Stored as `DOUBLE`; the model column validator caps the range at 0..1_000_000, and floats are allowed so callers can wedge a row between existing positions. The /reorder endpoint derives the stored value via `calculatePosition`.

schema.description: Link payload; creates a linked copy of the source element under the target container. Linked copies auto-sync from source until they are edited or explicitly unlinked.

get.summary: List content elements in the repository
get.description: Returns content elements, optionally narrowed to activity parents

schema.description: Restrict to elements whose `activityId` is in the provided set (the FE's bulk "load every element under these containers" call). Accepts a real array, a single value, or a comma-separated string.
Include detached elements (rows unreachable in the outline because an ancestor was deleted). Default: false.
Set false to include soft-deleted rows.

200.description: List of content elements matching the filter.

items.description: A content element inside a container activity. The element's `type` selects which extension package owns its `data` shape and lifecycle hooks.

post.description: Creates a content element under the scoped repository.

data.description: A content element inside a container activity. The element's `type` selects which extension package owns its `data` shape and lifecycle hooks.

type.description: Content-element type id, declared by an installed content-element extension package (e.g. `HTML`, `IMAGE`, `MULTIPLE_CHOICE`). Each package owns one type id; the elementRegistry dispatches lifecycle hooks and procedures by it. Capped at the model column length (`STRING` -> `VARCHAR(255)`).

data.description: Content element type defined property bag (JSONB). Shape is owned by the content element package's `ElementData` interface for this `type`.

meta.description: Meta-input data bag (JSONB). Schema-configured fields collected via meta inputs (captions, labels, and similar element-level config).

position.description: Position among siblings. Stored as `DOUBLE`; the model column validator caps the range at 0..1_000_000, and floats are allowed so callers can wedge a row between existing positions. The /reorder endpoint derives the stored value via `calculatePosition`.

refs.description: Cross-element references (JSONB). Keyed by the relationship types the schema declares for this element type via `elementMeta.relationships[]`; values are arrays of `Relationship` pointers. Remapped on deep-clone via `mapClonedReferences`.

isLinkedCopy.description: True when this element is an active linked copy of another; receives automatic updates when the source changes. Editing `data` auto-unlinks via the model's `autoUnlinkOnEdit` afterUpdate hook.

sourceId.description: Source element id this entry was copied from; preserved after unlink for provenance. Null when the source has been hard-deleted.

anyOf.description: Source `updatedAt` at the moment of last sync.

contentId.description: Origin id that survives clone/import so reuse and duplicates can be detected across repositories.

get.summary: Get a single content element by id

patch.description: Updates mutable fields; `deletedAt: null` restores the row.

meta.description: Meta-input data bag (JSONB). Schema-configured fields collected via meta inputs (captions, labels, and similar element-level config).

refs.description: Cross-element references (JSONB). Keyed by the relationship types the schema declares for this element type via `elementMeta.relationships[]`; values are arrays of `Relationship` pointers. Remapped on deep-clone via `mapClonedReferences`.

get.summary: Get cached review for an activity
get.description: Returns the cached review for an (activity, scoring rubric) pair, including staleness and in-flight run state.

schema.description: Scoring rubric; defaults to the schema default. Must also be enabled by the repository's schema.

200.description: Cached review state - status, staleness flag, last result, and recent score trend.

data.description: Cached review state for one (activity, rubric) pair.

post.description: Triggers a background review unless one is already running or the cached result matches the current content signature - safe to call from deferred triggers (idle-after-save, page-leave) without re-running on unchanged content. Returns the immediate status; poll the GET endpoint for completion.

200.description: Immediate review state, typically `running` when a new analysis was started.

data.description: Cached review state for one (activity, rubric) pair.

rubricId.description: Scoring rubric; defaults to the schema default. Must also be enabled by the repository's schema.

force.description: Re-run even when a fresh result exists. Used by the manual refresh button; deferred triggers omit it so unchanged content never re-runs.

get.summary: Time-travel an activity
get.description: Reconstructs an activity and its descendant element state at a target moment. Used by the FE PublishDiff view to show what was published.

schema.description: Target activity; the root of the subtree we reconstruct.
Strict ISO 8601 moment to reconstruct state at.

Element ids currently rendered by the caller. The server adds elements that were live at `timestamp` but have since been deleted (so content visible then but gone now is reconstructed) and returns each one's state as of `timestamp`.

200.description: Reconstructed activity + element revisions at the target moment.

data.description: Reconstructed state at the target moment, mirroring the Frontend PublishDiffProvider shape.

404.description: Activity not found in this repository.

200.description: Page of revisions for the repository.

schema.description: Add a presence context for the current user.

sseId.description: SSE connection id; populated when the context is bound to a live stream so server-side cleanup can target the right connection.

repositoryId.description: Repository the context is scoped to.

context.description: Context shape to match against the user’s stored contexts.

schema.description: Remove matching presence contexts for the current user.

post.description: Calls a named server procedure on the plugin for `type`. Payload is opaque; each procedure defines its own shape.

schema.description: Procedure name from the plugin's `procedures` map.
Opaque per-procedure payload; plugin owns the wire shape.
